projects.title: Historic Entry Door Restoration
projects.subtitle: Weld Mansion — Adams Arboretum Condominiums
description: 990 Centre Street, Jamaica Plain is the historic Weld Mansion — a landmark Victorian estate built around 1885 and converted into residential condominiums. The property sits on landscaped grounds directly adjacent to the Arnold Arboretum, one of the world's great botanical collections. The buildings retain much of their original architectural character, including the ornate carved hardwood entry doors that are among the most distinctive features of the estate.

Boston Door Company was engaged to restore five sets of entry doors across the two main buildings at 990 and 1000 Centre Street. All doors are original period hardwood, heavily carved in the Victorian manner, with arched glass panels and matching period hardware. Decades of deferred maintenance had left the finishes oxidized, checked, and in places completely failed.

The full scope covered complete abatement of existing finishes, wood damage repair, and 3–5 coats of Epifanes natural oil varnish on all exterior faces. Interior faces were cleaned, prepped, and topcoated throughout. All hardware was disassembled, serviced, and reinstalled; a broken hinge at the main 990 entry was repaired and matched to the original.

doors.label: 990 Centre St — Exterior Entry
doors.description: The main exterior double doors at 990 Centre Street — the most architecturally significant set on the property. Ornate carved hardwood with arched glass panels and original Victorian hardware. Finish had failed across most of the surface with visible weathering and checking in the wood. A broken hinge required repair and matching before reinstallation.
work: Complete abatement of existing exterior finish
Visible wood damage repaired
Exterior prepped and finished with Epifanes natural oil varnish — 3–5 coats
Interior face cleaned, prepped, and topcoated with natural oil varnish
Broken hinge repaired and matched to original
All hardware disassembled, maintained, and reinstalled
Doors reinstalled and full function verified

doors.label: 990 Centre St #3 — Exterior Entry
doors.description: The exterior entry doors serving unit #3 at 990 Centre Street — a second double door set on the same building, sharing the same period hardwood construction and arched glass detailing. Finish failure was similar in character to the main entry. Hardware was serviced in place.
work: Complete abatement of existing exterior finish
Visible wood damage repaired
Exterior prepped and finished with Epifanes natural oil varnish — 3–5 coats
Interior face cleaned, prepped, and topcoated with natural oil varnish
Hardware disassembled, maintained, and reinstalled
Doors reinstalled and full function verified

doors.label: 1000 Centre St — Exterior Entry
doors.description: The main exterior entry doors at 1000 Centre Street, the adjacent building on the Weld Mansion estate. A brick arch surround frames the double door set. Restoration scope matched the 990 exterior sets.
work: Complete abatement of existing exterior finish
Visible wood damage repaired
Exterior prepped and finished with Epifanes natural oil varnish — 3–5 coats

projects.title: Historic Door Restoration
projects.subtitle: MIT Campus — Cambridge, MA
projects.door_type: Historic painted wood with iron strap hinges
description: A landmark institutional entry on the MIT campus in Cambridge — large painted wood double doors set within a granite arch surround, fitted with original ornate iron strap hinges and period hardware. The doors had accumulated decades of paint buildup, surface damage, and hardware corrosion.

Boston Door Company restored the full entry: surface preparation, wood damage repair, hardware disassembly and servicing, and finish restoration. The distinctive ironwork strap hinges were cleaned, treated, and reinstalled to their original condition.

doors.label: MIT Campus — Exterior Entry
doors.description: Historic painted wood double doors with ornate iron strap hinges set in a granite arch surround. Decades of deferred maintenance had left the finish deteriorated and hardware corroded.
work: Complete surface preparation and paint restoration
Wood damage repaired
Iron strap hinges disassembled, cleaned, and reinstalled
Hardware serviced and reinstalled
Doors reinstalled and full function verified

projects.title: Historic Entry Door Restoration
projects.subtitle: 71 Marlborough Street — Back Bay
projects.door_type: Historic hardwood double entry with glass lights and brass kick plates
description: 71 Marlborough Street is a landmark Back Bay brownstone on one of Boston's most distinguished residential streets. The historic hardwood double entry features full-length glass lights, original brass kick plates, and a arched transom above — characteristic of the refined Federal-era rowhouse architecture of the neighborhood.

Boston Door Company restored the full entry. The existing finish had oxidized and failed across most of the surface, leaving the wood weathered and grey. The restoration brought the doors back to a rich, deep natural finish that reveals the quality of the original hardwood.

doors.label: 71 Marlborough St — Exterior Entry
doors.description: Historic hardwood double entry doors with full-length glass lights, brass kick plates, and arched transom. Finish had failed across the surface with significant weathering and oxidation.
work: Complete abatement of existing exterior finish
Exterior prepped and finished with natural oil varnish
Hardware serviced and reinstalled
Doors reinstalled and full function verified

services.title: Door Function
services.description: Adjustment, structural repairs, and weatherstripping. Full hardware services including replacement, selection, sourcing, and installation. Door installation included.

services.title: Front Door & Entryway Restoration
services.description: Museum-quality finish, complete restoration, finish maintenance, new construction finish, natural oil finish, and detailed fabrication for historic and period entry doors.

services.title: Consulting
services.description: Hardware guidance, finish selection, and restoration feasibility for homeowners, management companies, multi-unit buildings, and businesses.
